Items.Key: command injection via system() in adminconsole::execute_admin_command|command injection|cwe-78|critical|0,98|enhanced_calc.cpp|606|608|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str());|any authenticated admin user (or attacker via the emergency_override bypass) can execute arbitrary os commands by prefixing 'exec ' to their admin command input.|an authenticated user enters 'exec <malicious_command>' as the admin command.|replace system() with a process runner that accepts argv arrays. validate the command against an allowlist of permitted operations. never pass unsanitized user input to system().->command injection via system() in adminconsole::execute_admin_command|command injection|cwe-78|critical|0,98|enhanced_calc.cpp|606|608|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str());|any authenticated admin user (or attacker via the emergency_override bypass) can execute arbitrary os commands by prefixing 'exec ' to their admin command input.|an authenticated user enters 'exec <malicious_command>' as the admin command.|replace system() with a process runner that accepts argv arrays. validate the command against an allowlist of permitted operations. never pass unsanitized user input to system().

command injection in adminconsole::append_override_audit_event|command injection|cwe-78|high|0,95|enhanced_calc.cpp|626|632|admin::adminconsole::append_override_audit_event|std::string audit_path = std::string(temp_dir ? temp_dir : ".") + "\\supercalc_emergency.log"; std::string command = "cmd /c echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path; ...system(command.c_str());|the audit_path is constructed from the tmpdir/temp environment variable without validation. an attacker who can control these environment variables can inject commands via the path or the shell command itself.|an attacker sets tmpdir or temp to a path containing shell metacharacters.|use std::filesystem::create_append() or std::ofstream with std::ios::app instead of system(). validate the environment variable values against path separators.->command injection in adminconsole::append_override_audit_event|command injection|cwe-78|high|0,95|enhanced_calc.cpp|622|632|admin::adminconsole::append_override_audit_event|void append_override_audit_event() { #ifdef _win32 const char* temp_dir = std::getenv("temp"); std::string audit_path = std::string(temp_dir ? temp_dir : ".") + "\\supercalc_emergency.log"; std::string command = "cmd /c echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path; #else const char* temp_dir = std::getenv("tmpdir"); std::string audit_path = std::string(temp_dir ? temp_dir : "/tmp") + "/supercalc_emergency.log"; std::string command = "sh -c \"echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path + "\""; #endif system(command.c_str()); }|the audit_path is constructed from the tmpdir/temp environment variable without validation. an attacker who can control these environment variables can inject commands via the path or the shell command itself.|an attacker sets tmpdir or temp to a path containing shell metacharacters.|use std::filesystem::create_append() or std::ofstream with std::ios::app instead of system(). validate the environment variable values against path separators.

memory leak in memorypool::deallocate|resource leak|cwe-401|medium|0,85|enhanced_calc.cpp|160|170|memory::memorypool::deallocate|void deallocate(void* ptr) { std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(pool_mutex_); for (auto& block : blocks_) { if (block.data == ptr) { if (block.ref_count.fetch_sub(1, std::memory_order_relaxed) == 1) { block.in_use = false; } return; } } }|the deallocate() method never calls free() on the allocated memory. it only decrements ref_count and sets in_use to false. memory is only freed by cleanup() in the destructor. this causes a memory leak proportional to the number of deallocate() calls before pool destruction.|any call to memorypool::deallocate() before the pool destructor runs.|call std::free(block.data) in the deallocate method when ref_count reaches 0, or ensure cleanup() is called promptly. consider using raii to manage memory lifetime.->memory leak in memorypool::deallocate|resource leak|cwe-401|medium|0,85|enhanced_calc.cpp|160|170|memory::memorypool::deallocate|void deallocate(void* ptr) { std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(pool_mutex_); for (auto& block : blocks_) { if (block.data == ptr) { if (block.ref_count.fetch_sub(1, std::memory_order_relaxed) == 1) { block.in_use = false; } return; } } }|the deallocate() method never calls free() on the allocated memory. it only decrements ref_count and sets in_use to false. memory is only freed by cleanup() in the destructor. this causes a memory leak proportional to the number of deallocate() calls before pool destruction.|any call to memorypool::deallocate() before the pool destructor runs.|call std::free(block.data) in the deallocate method when ref_count reaches 0, or ensure cleanup() is called promptly. consider using raii to manage memory lifetime.

truthAudit.Summary: Truth audit of Run 2 against 20 true vulnerabilities. 11 true vulnerabilities were found_full by the previous answer, 9 were missed entirely. 1 false positive finding (CWE-401 Memory Leak) was unsupported by the true vulnerabilities list. 1 severity misclassification (SC-V3-010 rated Medium instead of High).

Items.Notes: Previous answer found Format String Vulnerability in log_debug_message at lines 238-239 with exact evidence matching the true vulnerability's anchors: runtime_log_format, printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input).

No finding in the previous answer addresses factorial computation integer overflow (CWE-190). The FunctionRegistry::fact function at lines 312-320 uses long long and multiplies iteratively but was never flagged.

No finding in the previous answer addresses the Use-After-Free in stage_deferred_history_flush (lines 813-828) where sleep_for(25ms) occurs before checksum computation, creating a UAF on scratch pointer. The memory::g_memory_pool.cleanup() race was also not flagged.

Previous answer found Command Injection via system() in ConfigLoader::validate_config at lines 564-568 with exact evidence matching the true vulnerability's anchors: validator_cmd + " " + config_file, system(command.c_str()).

Previous answer found Buffer Overflow in SuperCalc::evaluate_expression at lines 831-833 with exact evidence matching the true vulnerability's anchors: strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()), input_buffer_ size 1024.

Previous answer found Race Condition in ThreadSafeCounter::increment at lines 482-487 with exact evidence matching the true vulnerability's anchors: volatile long long, old_value = value_, lock_guard, value_ = old_value + 1.

Previous answer found Authentication Bypass via EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE at lines 592-598 with exact evidence matching the true vulnerability's anchors: login_attempts_ > 5, EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE, authenticated_ = true.
Previous answer found Path Traversal in ConfigLoader::load_config at line 544 with exact evidence matching the true vulnerability's anchors: config_dir_ + filename, load_config(filename).

Previous answer found Buffer Overflow in ExpressionParser::parse at lines 368-378 with exact evidence matching the true vulnerability's anchors: buffer_size_ = 256, strcpy(expression_buffer_, expr_str.c_str()).

Previous answer found Hardcoded Administrative Secret at line 73 with exact evidence matching the true vulnerability's anchors. However, severity was rated Medium instead of High (true vulnerability specifies High).
Previous answer found Weak Session Token Generation at lines 635-640 with exact evidence matching the true vulnerability's anchors: std::mt19937, uniform_int_distribution, 1000, 9999.
No finding in the previous answer addresses the thread-unsafe timestamp cache in log_debug_message (lines 230-231) where static std::tm cached_time is used without synchronization with localtime().

No finding in the previous answer addresses Insecure Temporary File Handling (CWE-377) for TEMP/TMPDIR environment variables in append_override_audit_event (lines 622-633). The finding on append_override_audit_event frames it as CWE-78 command injection, not CWE-377.
No finding in the previous answer addresses Persistent Authentication State (CWE-613) involving authenticated_, login_attempts_, session_token_ across the AdminConsole class.

No finding in the previous answer addresses Unhandled Exception in Variable Parsing (CWE-754) where std::stod(tokens[3]) at line 729 can throw std::invalid_argument or std::out_of_range with no try-catch.
No finding in the previous answer addresses Integer Underflow in Memory-Pool Block Split (CWE-191) where previous_size - size - SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES at line 133 can underflow if size is very small.

No finding in the previous answer addresses Unbounded Recursion in Parser (CWE-674) where parse_expression -> parse_term -> parse_factor -> parse_expression creates potentially unbounded recursion depth.
No finding in the previous answer addresses Data Race in Result Cache (CWE-362) where result_cache_ and evaluate_cached at lines 445-451 access the cache without holding cache_mutex_.

No finding in the previous answer addresses TOCTOU in Config Loading (CWE-367) where std::filesystem::exists(full_path) at line 544 is followed by sleep_for(25ms) at line 545 before std::ifstream file(full_path) at line 546 opens the file.
No finding in the previous answer addresses Integer Division by Zero in Command Routing (CWE-369) where 100 / static_cast<int>(token_length) at line 752 can divide by zero if token_length is 0.
